mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, discount rates and recession risk expectations decline, so S&P 500 prices tend to rise over the next month. The signal is confirmed if 20-day forward returns are stronger after rate-cutting sequences than after flat or rising policy paths.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than half of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens financial conditions enough to compress equity multiples and weaken near-term risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is not simultaneously falling.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction with CPIAUCSL adds no incremental predictive value versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE over recent months signals weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, which should pressure cyclical earnings expectations and lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is directional if unemployment is rising faster than payroll growth is slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not materially weaker after UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, or if the signal reverses sign when PAYEMS is stable or improving.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in RSAFS growth signals weaker consumer demand and revenue pressure for large-cap firms, which should reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The pattern is confirmed when retail sales deceleration precedes broad equity underperformance.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns do not fall below baseline after multi-month RSAFS deceleration, or if the relationship is absent in rolling 5-year sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO combined with weak HOUST indicates broad production softness and fading industrial activity, which should lower cyclical earnings expectations and depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when both series soften together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST fail to predict weaker 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint effect is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 indicates improving aggregate demand and higher expected corporate revenue, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relationship is validated when positive GDP momentum aligns with positive equity drift after macro releases.
failure_condition: Reject if higher GDPC1 growth does not correspond to stronger 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in PAYEMS above trend signals firm labor demand and household income support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week through better growth expectations. The signal is strongest when payroll gains are broad rather than isolated.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-strength triggers are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior month’s market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is falling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightens and equity duration is penalized, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if inflation deceleration is paired with immediate policy easing that offsets the real-rate pressure.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL declines for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined disinflation-plus-tight-policy regime does not predict below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the pattern is indistinguishable from the effect of CPIAUCSL alone.
